_meta.purpose: Captures the real operational environment, competitive dynamics, and strategic challenges at GQR that inform scheduling, job selection, and business strategy decisions
use_cases: Any conversation requiring understanding of Bryan's actual working environment

team_structure.team_dynamic: Internal competition for candidates when team roles drop

candidate_ownership_rules.first_contact_advantage: First recruiter to InMail a candidate owns submission rights for 48 hours
candidate_ownership_rules.typical_participation: When team role drops, 4-5 of 8 recruiters typically work it (not all 8)
candidate_ownership_rules.late_movers: 2-3 recruiters start at massive disadvantage because busy with other work when role dropped
candidate_ownership_rules.competition_intensity: Racing against teammates to identify and contact best candidates before they do

role_drop_dynamics.predictability: Completely unpredictable timing - could be any time during work hours
role_drop_dynamics.speculative_nature: Many roles are speculative (no official sign-off, no terms of business, limited information)
role_drop_dynamics.information_quality: Often working with: unclear salary ranges, unknown hiring managers, limited company knowledge
role_drop_dynamics.risk_of_waste: Can invest 2-3 hours sourcing → role evaporates → must clean up candidate relationships

examples.septerna_waste: Sourced 3 strong Clinical Dev candidates, spent hours on calls, TA found internal candidates, role closed. Deflating but would do again because it was in wheelhouse.
examples.structure_success: Multiple contract placements because client responsive, good relationship, fast process

information_asymmetry.owned_accounts: Full visibility - direct TA/hiring manager relationships, hear exact tone, no translation loss
information_asymmetry.team_accounts: Filtered visibility through account owner, information gets lost in translation, miss nuances
information_asymmetry.impact: Owned accounts = better information = higher win rate on placements
information_asymmetry.on_calls: Sometimes looking up company pipeline DURING candidate screening calls because info is so limited

team_roles.accounts: Structure, Nicole's Pathalys/Alkermes, other teammates' clients
team_roles.volume: Higher volume of opportunities because all team accounts available

owned_accounts.volume: Lower volume because only 4 accounts, still maturing
owned_accounts.note: Still compete with 7 teammates when owned account role drops, but Bryan gets full fee if he wins

strategic_math.scenario_a_all_team: 8 placements/year, all team roles at 50% = 8 × $12K = $96K commissions
strategic_math.insight: Same number of placements, 50% more money with owned account mix

project_setup.time_required: 60 minutes for LinkedIn Recruiter project setup
project_setup.activities: Boolean search, filters, target profiles, InMail template creation
project_setup.competitive_mode: Can streamline to 30-45 min if racing teammates, but quality suffers

inmail_outreach.time_per_batch: 30-60 minutes to review profiles and send 5-10 InMails
inmail_outreach.quality_vs_speed: Tension between thorough vetting and speed to claim candidates first

response_timeline.typical: Responses trickle in over first 72 hours
response_timeline.slow: Many take days, weeks, or months to respond
response_timeline.variability: Completely unpredictable - can't schedule 'screening calls this afternoon' from morning sourcing

screening_calls.scheduling: Back-and-forth to find time after InMail response
screening_calls.timing: Usually happen next week, not same day as sourcing
screening_calls.lagging_indicator: Screening calls completed are lagging indicator, InMails sent are leading indicator

activity_metrics.what_you_control: InMails sent, hiring manager calls attempted, projects created
activity_metrics.what_you_dont_control: Response rates, connection rates, screening call conversion
activity_metrics.kpi_measurement: Activity KPIs measure leading indicators (InMails sent), not lagging (calls completed)

factors_evaluated: Candidate desirability (will they want this role?)
Your competitive advantage (do you have an edge?)
Learning curve (how fast can you get competent?)

jump_immediately.criteria: Roles where Bryan has competitive advantage
examples: Structure contract roles (Aaron relationship, know process, recent placements)
CDM/Stat Programmer roles anywhere (fraud detection expertise, H1B/India verification techniques)
Clinical Development in companies Bryan knows well
Roles where Bryan recently placed candidates (process familiarity)
Contract roles generally (faster process, volume potential)

pass_or_token_effort.criteria: Roles with low candidate desirability or high difficulty
examples: Alkermes FTE roles (unrealistic expectations, hard sell, unpredictable rejections, 25 min max effort)
Roles requiring ultra-credentialed candidates (Harvard Med School types - uncomfortable probing)
Speculative roles with no TA contact (high waste risk like Septerna)
Roles where 3+ teammates already sourced heavily (too late to compete)

strategic_selective.criteria: Worth the risk but requires judgment
examples: Roles in wheelhouse but unfamiliar companies (Septerna Clinical Dev - worth the risk)
Roles outside wheelhouse but easy to learn + good client (Sr CTM at Structure)

structure_therapeutics.relationship: Strong relationship with account manager Aaron
structure_therapeutics.knowledge: Know hiring process, salary ceilings, team dynamics from recent placements
structure_therapeutics.responsiveness: Client very responsive, good visibility, fast decisions
structure_therapeutics.comfort: Can sell company well, understand pipeline, familiar with team

cdm_stat_programmer_roles.fraud_detection: Developed expertise spotting fraudulent candidates from India
cdm_stat_programmer_roles.h1b_identification: Strong gut instinct on H1B candidates before speaking to them
cdm_stat_programmer_roles.verification_techniques: Video call requirement, 'show me outside your window' technique to catch time zone fraud
cdm_stat_programmer_roles.innovation: Created solutions others aren't comfortable implementing
cdm_stat_programmer_roles.lower_skill_focus: Easier qualification process, focus on fraud detection vs deep technical probing

clinical_development_wheelhouse.pattern_recognition: Know what good candidates look like quickly
clinical_development_wheelhouse.limited_learning_curve: Can jump on roles in this area with minimal ramp-up

contract_roles_preference.volume: High-volume potential, can place multiple in short time
contract_roles_preference.efficiency: Less scrutiny, quicker decisions, better for activity metrics

areas_of_discomfort.ultra_credentialed_candidates: Uncomfortable probing Harvard Med School graduates, feels weird telling them they're not qualified
areas_of_discomfort.unfamiliar_therapeutic_areas: Steep learning curve, must research on the fly during calls
areas_of_discomfort.speculative_roles_no_ta: High risk of wasted time when no TA contact involved

ionis.status: Your account, 100% commission if you place
ionis.current_state: REACTIVATED - 2 remote roles (Safety Operations + Safety Specialist), 6 CV submissions, Anthony Elia has competing interview 2/17
ionis.contacts: Monique Burke (past work), Sanjay Gauthier (warm lead)
ionis.challenge: Interview scheduling delayed by mock inspection, need to lock down times before losing Anthony Elia to competing offer

aditum_bio.status: Your account, 100% commission if you place
aditum_bio.current_state: LOST - VP Clinical Development (Ben) lost to internal hire December 2025
aditum_bio.challenge: No active roles or relationship. Consider write-off unless new roles surface.

alumis.status: Your account, 100% commission if you place
alumis.current_state: Failed to sign terms of business
alumis.challenge: Stalled BD prospect, needs rescue or write-off

neurona.status: Your account, 100% commission if you place
neurona.current_state: Failed to sign terms of business
neurona.challenge: Stalled BD prospect, needs rescue or write-off

pathalys.status: Nicole's account, 50% split if Bryan places
pathalys.bryans_involvement: Contractors work there but Nicole owns relationship
pathalys.current_state: Active contractor extensions, potential Q1 pipeline

alkermes.status: Nicole's account, 50% split if Bryan places
alkermes.rationale: Hard sell, unrealistic expectations, unpredictable rejections, low ROI

xellar.bryans_performance: Dominated sourcing, had 3 backup candidates. Now rebuilding with Douglas Lee, Anirban Chatterjee, Michael Fenn (all sent resumes Feb 11-12)
xellar.outcome: Marcin (Bryan's) beaten by Kevin's candidates (Jan 2026). New candidates being submitted.

structure_therapeutics.bryans_performance: Multiple placements, strong relationship with Aaron (account manager)
structure_therapeutics.current_work: Senior CTM sourcing, competing with Rachel
structure_therapeutics.advantage: Bryan knows process, salary ranges, team dynamics from past placements

korro_bio.status: Trying to land as owned account
korro_bio.timeline: Jan 1, 2026 agency decision deadline PASSED - status unknown
korro_bio.strategy: Check status - deadline passed, need to determine if still viable

evolveimmune.status: Trying to land as owned account

axle.status: Trying to land as owned account
axle.current_state: Unresponsive to TOB + case studies, likely dead
axle.next_action: Jan 6, 2026 follow-up email if Q1 budgets approved

biotech_pharma_environment.unemployment: Historic low unemployment rates in biotech/pharma
biotech_pharma_environment.company_cash: Many companies cash-strapped, reluctant to pay agency fees
biotech_pharma_environment.hiring_difficulty: Mission-critical roles hard to fill but companies resist 20-30% contingent fees
biotech_pharma_environment.bd_challenge: Convincing cash-strapped companies to pay premium for talent they desperately need

bd_sales_cycle_reality.timeline: 6-12+ months to convert prospect to client (sometimes over a year)
bd_sales_cycle_reality.voicemail_strategy: Most calls = voicemail planting, callbacks come weeks/months later
bd_sales_cycle_reality.relationship_building: Volume game - 10 voicemails this week + 10 next week + follow-up emails = maybe 1 callback in January

bryans_year_2_context.challenge: Owned account portfolio still thin - Aditum lost, Alumis/Neurona stalled, Ionis just reactivated
bryans_year_2_context.green_key_losses: Lost 2 biggest accounts from previous firm to bankruptcies
bryans_year_2_context.current_bookings: ~$347,924 across 10 placements (FY25: April 1 - Feb 13 2026). Jan 2026: $61.5K Jake Olsen/IDEAYA. Feb 2026: $90,584 Liqiang He/IDEAYA.
bryans_year_2_context.progress: 10th placement booked (Liqiang He/IDEAYA $90.6K), best submission week ever (Feb 9), on track for all KPIs mid-month, IDEAYA became biggest volume driver (2 placements in 5 weeks), Ionis reactivated with 2 roles, webinar panel completed

strategic_positioning.current_state: Year 2 - Ionis reactivated, IDEAYA high-volume driver, Aditum lost, BD sequences active for Q1 2026

structural_tension.short_term_survival: Win enough team competitions to hit activity KPIs and stay employed
structural_tension.long_term_wealth: Build owned account portfolio where placements = 2x the commission
structural_tension.time_allocation_reality: Can't avoid team competition (unpredictable role drops), must maintain contribution

impossible_strategies.protected_time_blocks: Cannot schedule 2-hour 'protected sourcing blocks' for owned accounts when team roles drop unpredictably
impossible_strategies.avoiding_team_roles: Cannot just 'focus on owned accounts' - would miss KPIs and create political problems
impossible_strategies.forcing_50_50_split: Structure prevents clean 50/50 owned vs team allocation

realistic_strategies.minimum_viable_owned_account_maintenance: 15-30 min relationship touches 3x/week on owned accounts
realistic_strategies.selective_team_competition: Jump on roles where Bryan has competitive advantage, pass on Alkermes-type hard sells
realistic_strategies.rapid_owned_account_response: When Ionis/Aditum roles drop, compete HARD because 100% commission justifies effort
realistic_strategies.bd_pipeline_building: Focus BD efforts on landing 1-2 accounts that become exclusive producers
realistic_strategies.leverage_advantages: Play to Structure relationship, CDM fraud detection, Clinical Dev wheelhouse

two_track_approach.track_1_short_term: Win more team competitions by playing to strengths (Structure, contract roles, CDM)
two_track_approach.track_2_long_term: Minimum viable owned account maintenance + aggressive BD on Korro/EvolveImmune

what_doesnt_work: Fixed morning blocks for 'owned account sourcing' (team roles will interrupt)
Scheduling 'sourcing + screening calls' same day (responses take days/weeks)
Planning perfect 50/50 split days (structure makes this impossible)
Ignoring team role drops to focus on strategy (political suicide + misses KPIs)
what_does_work: Flexible afternoon blocks that pivot based on morning activity (team role dropped? Korro responses came in?)
Decision points at 10 AM and 1 PM to assess priorities
Morning time for owned account relationship touches before team online
Minimum viable owned account maintenance (30-60 min/day) rather than 2-hour blocks
Building in buffer/flex time for unpredictable team role drops
Measuring success by activities completed (InMails sent, calls attempted) not conversions (screens completed)

realistic_time_allocation.owned_accounts_bd: 35-45% (impossible to get higher given team role drops)
realistic_time_allocation.acceptance: This is Year 1 reality, not inefficiency. Allocation improves as owned accounts mature.

nicole_leinders.dynamic: Her priorities (team success, her accounts) don't perfectly align with Bryan's individual success
nicole_leinders.bryans_approach: Must appear cooperative and hit team KPIs while building own account portfolio
nicole_leinders.meeting_prep: Always prepare with specific data, solutions-oriented questions, track promises

aaron_structure.value: Responsive, good visibility, Bryan's strongest client relationship
aaron_structure.advantage: Bryan knows salary ceilings, process, team dynamics
aaron_structure.why_important: Placements here are high-probability because of relationship depth

teammates_competition.rachel: Also sourcing Structure Sr CTM, ahead in some searches
teammates_competition.aaron_holly_others: All competing for same candidate pool when team roles drop
teammates_competition.dynamic: Cooperative team culture BUT internal competition is real

evaluate: Is this in my wheelhouse? (Clinical Dev, CDM, contract roles)
Do I have relationship advantage? (Structure, companies I know)
Is candidate profile desirable? (Good sell, realistic expectations)
Is client responsive? (Will I get feedback or waste time?)
Am I early enough to compete? (Or are 3+ teammates already sourcing?)
when_team_role_drops.pass_if: Alkermes-type hard sell OR no competitive edge OR already late to race

owned_account_prioritization.ionis: Relationship cultivation focus - hiring manager outreach, not just open role hunting
owned_account_prioritization.aditum: Breakthrough or closure needed - stop limbo state
owned_account_prioritization.korro_bd: Highest urgency - Jan 1 deadline, build candidate value before decision
owned_account_prioritization.evolveimmune_bd: Medium priority - Q1 buildout timing, relationship cultivation
owned_account_prioritization.axle_bd: Low priority - likely dead, Q1 check-in only

time_investment_decisions.high_roi: Structure roles (relationship + 50% split), Owned account roles (100% split), CDM fraud detection roles (competitive advantage)
time_investment_decisions.medium_roi: Team roles in wheelhouse (50% split but good win probability)
time_investment_decisions.low_roi: Alkermes (token effort only), Speculative roles without TA contact (high waste risk)
time_investment_decisions.when_uncertain: Default to: Do I have competitive advantage? If no, pass.

for_nicole_meetings.frame_team_contribution: Structure placements, CDM roles, team sourcing for activity KPIs
for_nicole_meetings.frame_owned_account_progress: Relationship touches completed, BD pipeline advancement (Korro, EvolveImmune)
for_nicole_meetings.explain_allocation: Team role drops are unpredictable, maintaining contribution while building owned accounts
for_nicole_meetings.data_to_track: InMails sent, calls attempted (leading indicators) not just placements (lagging)
for_nicole_meetings.avoid: Don't complain about team competition or express frustration with structural constraints

success_metrics.bookings: ~$347,924 FY25 across 10 placements (Jan 2026: +$61.5K Jake Olsen/IDEAYA, Feb 2026: +$90,584 Liqiang He/IDEAYA)
success_metrics.average_placement: $34,792 (elevated due to contract strategy - 2 IDEAYA placements in 5 weeks)
success_metrics.pipeline: 15+ active opportunities across IDEAYA (6), Structure (5), Xellar (1), Ionis (2), Iona (1), Carrie Farm (2), Karyopharm (3)

critical_insights.this_is_not_time_management: Bryan's challenge isn't managing time better - it's strategic positioning in Year 1 of account building during worst biotech market in decade
critical_insights.structural_constraints: The competitive team dynamic, unpredictable role drops, and 50% commission split on team roles create structural barriers to clean time allocation
critical_insights.year_1_reality: Owned accounts aren't mature enough to generate sufficient volume. BD prospects take 6-12 months to convert. Team support fills the gap and hits KPIs.
critical_insights.the_trap: Reactive loop = team role drops → race teammates → invest hours → role evaporates OR someone else places → clean up candidates → repeat. Meanwhile owned accounts starve for attention.
critical_insights.the_escape: Minimum viable owned account maintenance + selective team competition (play to strengths) + aggressive BD on highest-potential prospects (Korro Jan 1 deadline)
critical_insights.success_looks_like: Year 2: 60% of placements from owned accounts (100% commission) because accounts matured. Year 1: 70% team support is survival mode, not failure.
critical_insights.what_ai_scheduling_cant_solve: Cannot create 'protected time blocks' when team roles drop unpredictably. Cannot force 50/50 allocation when structure prevents it. Cannot schedule same-day screening calls from morning sourcing.
critical_insights.what_ai_scheduling_can_solve: Flexible decision frameworks, realistic success metrics (activities not conversions), strategic prioritization of owned account touches, minimum viable maintenance approach
